heading: Rights of declarants; participation in actions, awards
label: 4225 Rights of declarants; participation in actions, awards
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (d), probably is a reference to former , which was repealed by , , .Section 3509A of title 18section 3059A of title 18Pub. L. 107–273, div. A, title III, § 301(c)(2)116 Stat. 1781Nov. 2, 2002
amendments: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (d), probably is a reference to former , which was repealed by , , .Section 3509A of title 18section 3059A of title 18Pub. L. 107–273, div. A, title III, § 301(c)(2)116 Stat. 1781Nov. 2, 2002

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A person who has filed a declaration that meets the requirements of sections 4221 through 4224 of this title shall have the rights stated in this section.

children.heading: Civil action
children.label: b Civil action
children.text: If the Attorney General determines that a proceeding to recover the asset or assets identified in the declaration should be referred to private counsel pursuant to subchapter III, the declarant, after consultation with the Attorney General, shall have the right to select counsel to prosecute the action, and the declarant and the declarant’s counsel shall act in accordance with subchapter III.

children.heading: Share of assets
children.label: c Share of assets
children.text: When the United States recovers any asset or assets specifically identified in a valid declaration filed under and the Attorney General determines that the asset or assets would not have been recovered if the declaration had not been filed, the declarant shall have the right to share in the recovery in the amount of 20 percent to 30 percent of any recovery up to the first $1,000,000 recovered, 10 percent to 20 percent of the next $4,000,000 recovered, and 5 percent to 10 percent of the next $5,000,000 recovered.section 4221 of this title

No person shall receive both an award under this section and a reward under either or section 3509A of title 18 for providing the same or substantially similar information.section 1831k of this title1See References in Text note below.1

When a person qualifies for both an award under this section and a reward under either or section 3509A of title 18 for providing the same or substantially similar information, the person may notify the Attorney General in writing of the person’s election to seek an award under this section or a reward under such other section.section 1831k of this title1

children.heading: Appropriate Federal banking agency exception
children.label: e Appropriate Federal banking agency exception
children.text: For purposes of this section, funds or assets acquired by the United States shall not include any funds or assets acquired by any appropriate Federal banking agency acting in any capacity or the Resolution Trust Corporation acting in any capacity, except for any civil money penalties recovered by a Federal banking agency through a final judgement, order, or settlement.
